title: TECHNICAL COORDINATOR – ENERGIA & I 4.0
description:
ICIM SpA, ente di certificazione accreditato dal 1988, è da sempre impegnato nel diffondere la cultura della conformità normativa come valore per aziende di ogni settore e dimensione.


Appartiene a **ICIM Group,** una holding che, attraverso diverse società, propone alle imprese servizi di **consulenza, formazione**, **testing** e **certificazione**: soluzioni tra loro integrate per aumentare la competitività delle aziende sui mercati globali, per far crescere il business in ottica di **innovazione** e **sostenibilità** e per garantire al mercato prodotti sicuri e conformi.

**Opportunità:**


Per il Dipartimento Tecnico di ICIM SpA dedicato alle certificazioni in ambito **Energia \& Transizione Tecnologica** ricerchiamo un **Technical Coordinator.**

  



  

In questo ruolo ti occuperai in particolar modo del coordinamento/svolgimento delle seguenti attività:

* Riesame delle pratiche di verifica e della documentazione tecnica dei clienti
* Conduzione di attività di audit come valutatore/ispettore qualificato dall’organismo di certificazione in vari settori della certificazione cogente e volontaria
* Formazione tecnica ed aggiornamento dei valutatori/ispettori qualificati nel settore di riferimento
* Aggiornamento e redazione di procedure gestionali ed operative per la regolamentazione delle attività tecniche e commerciali da parte dell’organismo di certificazione
* Gestione e mantenimento degli accreditamenti dell’ organismo di certificazione in collaborazione con il Responsabile.


* **Requisiti:**


	+ Laurea in Ingegneria preferibilmente Energetica o affini
	+ Conoscenza degli schemi **ISCC EU**
	+ Conoscenza del **Sistema nazionale di certificazione della sostenibilità dei biocarburanti e dei bioliquidi**
	+ Gradita esperienza e conoscenza dei processi aziendali di un **Organismo di Certificazione**
	+ Conoscenza del mercato **TIC\+C** e dei principali regolamenti e norme nazionali e internazionali in materia di accreditamento e notifica
	+ Capacità di pianificazione, gestione delle priorità e rispetto delle scadenze


* **Completano il profilo i seguenti requisiti ideali:**
* + Conoscenza del Regolamento (UE) 2024/573
	+ Conoscenza dello schema HP Keymark per le pompe di calore
	+ Conoscenza della legislazione relativa alle attestazioni di conformità ai sensi del Piano Transizione 4\.0

*In ottemperanza al d.lgs. 198/2006, la ricerca è rivolta a persone di entrambi i sessi.*

title: Junior Digital Marketing Specialist
description:
Profilo  
**Vivavoce cerca una figura junior da inserire nel team marketing per supportare lo sviluppo delle attività di digital advertising e acquisizione lead.** La persona lavorerà sui progetti Vivavoce e HeyUP, affiancando il team nella gestione operativa delle campagne, nell’analisi dei dati e nella ricerca di nuove opportunità di crescita digitale.

**Cerchiamo un profilo all’inizio del proprio percorso, ma con mentalità analitica, curiosità per le piattaforme digitali e voglia di imparare sul campo.** Il ruolo è pensato per una persona che voglia crescere nel digital marketing lavorando su campagne reali, funnel, creatività, landing page e test di acquisizione.

Chi siamo

**Vivavoce** è un ecosistema specializzato nella voce, nel linguaggio e nella comunicazione, con eccellenza nella balbuzie. Integra attività clinica, ricerca scientifica, sviluppo tecnologico e iniziative culturali.

**HeyUP** è il progetto di psicologia online dedicato ad adolescenti, genitori e famiglie. Entrambi i progetti lavorano su bisogni reali e richiedono una comunicazione digitale accurata, misurabile e rispettosa dei temi trattati.

Il ruolo

**La figura supporterà le attività di advertising su Google, Meta e TikTok, con un approccio pratico e data\-driven.** Non è richiesta un’esperienza senior, ma è importante avere interesse concreto per campagne, dati, creatività, target, landing page e qualità dei lead generati.

**Una parte del lavoro sarà dedicata anche alla ricerca di canali e iniziative di acquisizione non convenzionali:** partnership, community, creator, newsletter, micro\-iniziative territoriali o digitali, collaborazioni e nuovi modi per intercettare pubblici rilevanti.

Cosa farai

· Supporterai la gestione operativa di campagne Google Ads, Meta Ads e TikTok Ads.

· Aiuterai a monitorare metriche come CTR, CPC, CPL, conversioni e qualità dei contatti generati.

· Collaborerai alla preparazione di annunci, creatività, copy e varianti di test.

· Analizzerai keyword, search terms, pubblici, placement e performance delle campagne.

· Supporterai il controllo della coerenza tra annunci, landing page e bisogno dell’utente.

· Preparerai report sintetici per capire cosa funziona, cosa non funziona e cosa testare dopo.

· Farai ricerca su competitor, benchmark, trend e canali di acquisizione alternativi.

· Contribuirai alla sperimentazione di attività digitali non convenzionali per generare lead qualificati.

Chi cerchiamo

**Cerchiamo una persona junior, alla prima esperienza o con una breve esperienza in marketing digitale, advertising, comunicazione o analisi dati.** Può arrivare da percorsi in marketing, comunicazione, economia, digital media, data analysis o da esperienze personali su progetti online.

**Non cerchiamo un profilo già formato in ogni dettaglio.** Cerchiamo una persona precisa, curiosa, veloce nell’imparare e capace di ragionare sui numeri senza perdere di vista le persone che ci sono dietro quei numeri.

Cosa ci piacerebbe trovare in te

· interesse concreto per Google Ads, Meta Ads, TikTok Ads e performance marketing;

· buona capacità di leggere dati, tabelle e report senza fermarsi alle metriche superficiali;

· precisione operativa e attenzione ai dettagli;

· curiosità verso funnel, landing page, creatività, copy e comportamento degli utenti;

· buona dimestichezza con Excel/Google Sheets e strumenti digitali di base;

· capacità di fare ricerca online in modo ordinato e intelligente;

· voglia di proporre test, imparare dagli errori e migliorare rapidamente.

Cosa apprezziamo

**Sono utili, ma non obbligatorie, prime esperienze con campagne online, Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, reportistica, CRM, newsletter, creator marketing o attività di lead generation.** Apprezziamo anche progetti personali, piccoli esperimenti digitali, pagine social gestite, analisi fatte per studio o passione, oppure esperienze in contesti imprenditoriali.

Inserimento

**Il percorso di inserimento sarà definito in base al profilo e all’esperienza.** Valutiamo stage curriculare o extracurriculare per profili alla prima esperienza, oppure apprendistato per profili junior che abbiano già maturato prime esperienze o mostrino un potenziale particolarmente forte.

**Sede di riferimento: Milano.** La modalità sarà definita in base al profilo, con momenti in presenza utili per lavorare vicino al team marketing, al team clinico e al founder.

Come candidarti

**Mandaci CV o profilo LinkedIn, una breve presentazione e, se li hai, esempi di attività o progetti digitali a cui hai lavorato.** Insieme alla candidatura, rispondi anche a questa domanda:

*“Una campagna genera molti click ma pochi contatti qualificati: quali sono le prime tre cose che controlleresti?”*

**Non cerchiamo una risposta scolastica. Cerchiamo il tuo modo di ragionare, osservare i dati e impostare un test.**

Retribuzione: €243,62 \- €2\.369,36 al mese

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Supervisore del Laboratorio Microbiologico del Controllo Qualità
description:
**Data:** 15 mag 2026
**Dipartimento:** Corporate Quality Operations
**Business Area:** Quality
**Tipo Lavoro:** Dipendente
**Tipologia Contratto:** Tempo indeterminato
**Località:**milano, IT
***Chi siamo***

Chiesi è un gruppo biofarmaceutico internazionale focalizzato sulla ricerca, con 90 anni di esperienza e presente in oltre 30 Paesi, con sede centrale a Parma, Italia. Più di 8\.000 collaboratori in tutto il gruppo sono uniti da un unico obiettivo: promuovere un mondo più sano per le persone, i pazienti e il pianeta. Questo è ciò che ci guida nella ricerca, nello sviluppo e nella commercializzazione di farmaci innovativi nelle nostre principali aree terapeutiche. Scopri di più qui.

In Chiesi pensiamo alle generazioni future, promuovendo un’innovazione sostenibile e orientata allo scopo. Questo alimenta una cultura di affidabilità, trasparenza e comportamento etico a tutti i livelli. In quanto Società Benefit e certificata B Corp, abbiamo integrato la sostenibilità nel nostro statuto e misuriamo costantemente il nostro impatto.

Diversità e inclusione sono al cuore della nostra identità. Crediamo che le nostre differenze ci rendano più forti. Siamo un ecosistema dinamico di persone appassionate e di talento, unite da forti valori, ognuna delle quali porta prospettive uniche che ci aiutano a sfidare continuamente lo status quo in meglio.
***Global Technical Operations \& Supply***

Global Technical Operations \& Supply (Global TechOp\&S) è l’area globale di Chiesi responsabile della produzione e della supply chain, con l’obiettivo di garantire qualità, servizio e valore costanti ed eccellenti lungo l’intera catena di fornitura end\-to\-end. Global TechOp\&S supervisiona l’intero ciclo di vita del farmaco: dall’ottimizzazione dei processi e dall’industrializzazione, alla produzione, all’ingegneria, alla supervisione della qualità e alla gestione della supply chain. Durante tutto questo percorso, l’Operational Excellence guida il miglioramento continuo. Questo modello end\-to\-end è supportato dai nostri quattro siti produttivi \- Parma (Italia), Blois (Francia), Santana de Parnaíba (Brasile) e Nerviano (Italia) \- insieme alla nostra rete di Virtual Plant composta da partner esterni.**Ecco cosa farai**

Il ruolo ha la responsabilità di garantire la corretta esecuzione dei controlli microbiologici, coordinando le attività del laboratorio nel rispetto delle normative GMP (EU e US), delle linee guida regolatorie e degli standard qualitativi aziendali.**Sarai responsabile di**
* Pianificare e organizzare le attività del laboratorio, monitorando avanzamento e priorità.
* Coordinare il team di analisti e supervisionare l’esecuzione e la verifica delle analisi microbiologiche.
* Garantire la corretta gestione e taratura degli strumenti di laboratorio.
* Assicurare la gestione accurata della documentazione analitica e dei risultati (data integrity).
* Curare l’emissione dei certificati/bollettini di analisi.
* Contribuire alla formazione tecnica del personale.
* Redigere e aggiornare procedure operative nel rispetto delle normative GMP.
* Vigilare sull’applicazione degli standard qualitativi e delle procedure aziendali.
* Supportare la gestione e lo sviluppo del team insieme al Responsabile di reparto.
**Dovrai avere**
* Laurea in discipline scientifiche (Biologia, Biotecnologie o affini).
* Esperienza di almeno 5 anni in laboratorio microbiologico farmaceutico (ambienti sterili/asepsi).
* Conoscenza delle tecniche analitiche e dei principi di data integrity (ALCOA\+).
* Buone capacità organizzative, comunicative e di team working.
* Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.
**Località**

Nerviano, Milano***Cosa offriamo***

Ovunque inizi il tuo percorso in Chiesi, ti porterà a possibilità stimolanti. Il tuo futuro è al centro e ci impegniamo a sostenere il tuo sviluppo in un ambiente dinamico e amichevole, con accesso a risorse e formazione in ogni fase.

Offriamo benefit di alto livello, inclusi programmi completi di assistenza sanitaria, iniziative per l’equilibrio vita\-lavoro e supporto robusto per il trasferimento. Il pacchetto retributivo è competitivo e comprende salario base, bonus di performance e benefit allineati al mercato esterno. Inoltre, offriamo flessibilità lavorativa, opzioni di lavoro da remoto e servizi fiscali per colleghi internazionali, progettati per aiutarti a prosperare.
*Chiesi è un datore di lavoro che offre pari opportunità e si impegna ad assumere una forza lavoro diversificata a tutti i livelli. Tutti i candidati qualificati vengono considerati per l’impiego in base all’attitudine e al merito, senza riguardo a razza, origine nazionale, età, sesso, religione, disabilità, orientamento sessuale, stato civile, stato militare, identità o espressione di genere o qualsiasi altro criterio protetto dalla legge.*
SCOPRI TUTTE LE NOSTRE POSIZIONI APERTE

title: Commercial Strategy Manager
description:
Commercial Strategy Manager



CHI SIAMO



Da oltre 50 anni HEINEKEN è leader del settore birrario in Italia, con una quota di mercato del 33,3% e oltre 2\.000 collaboratori su tutto il territorio nazionale. Produciamo più di 6 milioni di ettolitri di birra ogni anno nei nostri quattro birrifici situati a Comun Nuovo (BG), Pollein (AO), Massafra (TA) e Assemini (CA), a cui si aggiunge il contributo fondamentale di Partesa, la nostra rete di distribuzione e servizi dedicata al canale Ho.Re.Ca.


Grazie a una solida identità locale e una presenza globale, HEINEKEN unisce innovazione e tradizione, producendo un impatto positivo sulle comunità in cui opera.



In HEINEKEN promuoviamo un ambiente di lavoro inclusivo, collaborativo e stimolante, valorizzando le persone attraverso percorsi di crescita, formazione, welfare e opportunità di lavoro flessibile.


Abbracciare e celebrare la diversità è fondamentale per il Purpose di HEINEKEN: "Creare il piacere di stare insieme per ispirare un mondo migliore". Riteniamo che le persone, diverse come le nostre birre, anche e non solo per genere, etnia, età, orientamento sessuale, credo religioso, nazionalità, estrazione sociale, disabilità e stile di pensiero, arricchiscano le nostre vite e rafforzino la nostra attività. Accogliamo e prendiamo in considerazione le candidature di tutte le persone qualificate, in quanto sosteniamo una cultura di appartenenza che offre opportunità eque e paritarie per tuttə.



LA POSIZIONE E IL TEAM



HEINEKEN Italia è alla ricerca di una risorsa da inserire nell’HQ di Sesto San Giovanni nel team Trade Marketing. Il/la Commercial Strategy Manager che avrà la responsabilità di definire la strategia commerciale e d'attivazione del canale Horeca. Gestendo un team di 3 risorse, il/la Commercial Strategy Manager andrà a sfruttare i dati provenienti da Commercial Insights per definire i cardini della strategia di canale andando a costruire e comunicare gli strumenti necessari alla forza vendita per l'esecuzione. Definirà, inoltre, gli strumenti che il team Channel Activation dovrà sviluppare e per essere poi implementati a punto vendita.



SEDE DI LAVORO: Sesto San Giovanni



FUNZIONE: Horeca



ATTIVITA’:


* Definire ed assicurare l'implementazione della strategia Route\-to\-Market del canale Horeca, segmentando i clienti a livello nazionale e regionale, definendo ruoli e contact strategy ed assegnando coerentemente obiettivi di P\&L; su ciascun cliente e ciascuna marca.
* Guidare il processo di Strategic Planning e di Annual Planning rappresentando il canale Horeca, identificando priorità chiave e definendo obiettivi (per brand, main pack, geografia) in linea con la strategia della OpCo.
* Definizione della politica incentivi forza vendita e monitoraggio dell'esecuzione sul canale Horeca.
* Definizione della routine della forza vendita Sell In e Sell Out e costruzione degli strumenti (es. Salesforce) per automatizzarne il monitoraggio.
* Definizione della politica prezzi (prezzo netto per cliente, per SKU) su tutto il territorio nazionale, della politica sconti a livello consortile e gestione delle approvazioni delle proposte di sconti periferici provenienti dalla forza vendita.
* Definizione della Picture of Success (PICOS) a livello nazionale, comunicazione alla forza vendita, implementazione a sistema degli strumenti automatici per comunicarla in maniera efficace a livello di singolo punto di consumo (es. Salesforce) in partnership con il team Go To Market.
* Politica assortimentale a livello regione e sottocanale, touch point consumatore da attivare a livello di punto di consumo secondo i criteri d'importanza dello stesso (immagine/volume), strategia di costruzione visibilità di marca per sottocanale.
* Definizione degli strumenti necessari per la realizzazione del piano annuale di marca, integrato nel piano canale (Growth Plan) e relativa allocazione del budget BTL, finalizzata alla realizzazione da parte delle altre sottofunzioni degli strumenti necessari alla realizzazione del piano, nelle tempistiche e modalità ideali per far coincidere le ways of working aziendali e le esigenze di mercato.

REQUISITI:


* Hai un’ottima conoscenza delle meccaniche commerciali
* Hai una profonda conoscenza degli strumenti, dei materiali e delle tecniche di Trade Marketing
* Hai un’ottima conoscenza dei mercati e dei canali distributivi
* Hai ottime capacità di Project Management
* Hai una buona conoscenza delle strategie promozionali
* Hai ottime capacità relazionali e di analisi
* Hai una buona conoscenza della lingua inglese
* Hai un'ottima conoscenza del pacchetto Microsoft Office


Perché lavorare con noi?



Entrare in HEINEKEN Italia significa far parte di una realtà dinamica, internazionale e in continua evoluzione, dove ogni persona ha l’opportunità di fare la differenza.  

Crediamo nella libertà di esprimere idee, nel valore della collaborazione e nello sviluppo continuo delle competenze individuali.  

Ci impegniamo ogni giorno per costruire un ambiente inclusivo, sostenibile e appassionante, dove il lavoro ha un impatto reale e ogni giornata è un’occasione per imparare, contribuire e divertirsi.



Segui la stella – scopri HEINEKEN e preparati a brillare ogni giorno, iniziando con energia e terminando con soddisfazione.

title: Tecnico Processi di Misura
description:
Con 85 anni di esperienza nella realizzazione e gestione di infrastrutture del gas naturale, Snam è un operatore leader a livello europeo in tre principali ambiti di business: il trasporto, di cui detiene il primato con oltre 38\.000 km di rete in Italia e all’estero, lo stoccaggio, con un sesto dell’intera capacità dell’Unione Europea, e la rigassificazione, di cui è oggi il terzo player europeo, con una capacità annua gestita (o co\-gestita) pari a 28 miliardi di metri cubi.


Tra le principali società quotate italiane per capitalizzazione di mercato, Snam garantisce la sicurezza degli approvvigionamenti del Paese attraverso il proprio sistema di infrastrutture, abilita l’integrazione energetica fra diversi vettori e promuove progetti di decarbonizzazione funzionali alla competitività delle imprese e allo sviluppo dei territori.


**Tecnico Processi di Misura**

  


**Sei pronto a trasformare le tue passioni in un percorso di sviluppo continuo?**

  



Come professionista Tecnico Processi di Misura, sarai una figura chiave nel nostro impegno quotidiano per l’ottimizzazione del servizio di trasporto e stoccaggio del gas. Farai parte della direzione Dispacciamento e Misura, nello specifico nell’area Processi di Misura all’interno del team Gestione Impianti di Misura, e avrai la responsabilità di relazionarti sia con i Line Manager sia con le funzioni specialistiche, occupandoti dell’attività di manutenzione degli impianti di misura di Snam Rete Gas.

  




**Perché dovresti considerare di unirti a noi?**

  



 Sfida strategica: Collabora direttamente con i manager aziendali per sviluppare e implementare strategie e soluzioni innovative nel settore dell'energia.



 Crescita professionale: avrai l'opportunità di guidare progetti complessi e influenzare il cambiamento organizzativo in un ambiente dinamico.

Operational Supply Planning Professional (m/f/d)
description:
**Operational Supply Planning Professional (m/f/d)**  

  

  

  

**Operational Supply Planning Professional (m/f/d)**  



The objective of BHC Manufacturing S.r.l. is to manufacture pharmaceutical products for the world market; the company is integrated within Bayer's Pharmaceuticals Division. The company operates through the manufacturing site located in Garbagnate Milanese (MI), where pharmaceuticals in solid form such as tablets and granules (microcapsules) are produced, packaged and released.



In this context, which is characterized by significant complexities (e.g. international scope, size and variety of equipment under management) the responsibilities of the role in scope are to ensure compliance with relevant standards while efficiently planning short\-term production. The role aligns production of tablets and granules with resource availability, involves monitoring production schedules, tracking output, and addressing exceptions to meet demand. OMP and SAP as well as further digital tools are used for planning. The role supports decision\-making and drives process improvements to optimize manufacturing operations and achieve supply chain objectives.

  


**YOUR TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**  


* Driving site activities to guarantee compliance with GMP, ISO (14001 and 9001\), OHSAS 18001 and internal company regulations and quality standards
* Ensuring efficient short\-term bulk planning using OMP, SAP ERP \& other digital planning tools to satisfy the finished product demand with regards to due dates and quantities
* Alignment of short\-term bulk plan with resource availability
* Production execution (e.g. orders approval) and monitoring of production plan, tracking of plan vs. actual output on weekly and monthly basis in terms volume, shifts, and planned/unplanned downtimes
* Exception cooperation/steering, including bottleneck/risk management with the internal organization, order changes due to unplanned demand, bridging stocks, site shutdowns, change management, launches etc.
* Dependent demand management for Garbagnate semi\-finished product within short\-term horizon (four months)
* Support in decision making process within planned scenarios
* Change management alignment (e.g., launches, new train of equipment)
* Support of master data maintenance, alignment of production times, change\-over times, validated campaigns, lead times for quality release and production picking and packing in the warehouse
* Preparations of weekly planning meeting with Production and Quality
* Support of an effective release plan for finished product, including Bulk sales to Bayer affiliates
* Standardization and efficiency improvement through constant review of KPI and support of improvement measures
* Key systems: OMP, SAP ERP
* Preferably experience in PowerBI or Python / similar programming language

  


**WHO YOU ARE:**


* University degree in supply chain management, business studies, computer science, industrial engineering, economics or an equivalent qualification / work experience
* Relevant operational experience (\> 5 years) within supply chain management, manufacturing, procurement, or quality in the life science or FMCG industry (international exposure would be a plus)
* Familiarity with production processes \& technology and their role in the overall supply chain context
* Understanding requirements of legal, regulatory and GMP/GXP environment
* Demonstrated success in delivering results in complex environment
* Strong communication skills to effectively convey information to various stakeholders across hierarchical levels and functions
* Skilled at building and maintaining relationships with various stakeholders to achieve shared goals
* Working effectively with cross\-functional teams in supply chain management
* Analytical mindset and focus on excellence and continuous improvement
* Understanding and expertise in using supply chain planning software (preferably OMP)
* Preferably English language proficiency

  





Location: Garbagnate Milanese (MI)

Type of Contract: Permanent

Italy : Lombardia : Garbagnate Milanese

Pharmaceuticals

description:
Milan, Italy \| Full time \| Home\-based \| R1545592
  
**Job Overview**
The Associate Medical Director will provide medical, clinical and scientific advisory expertise to all IQVIA divisions as requested. Participate in all aspects of Medical Science involvement on assigned trials. Serve as a medical expert during project delivery lifecycle. Provide therapeutic and medical expertise to business development activities.**Essential Functions***Medical Monitoring:** Primarily serves as Global Medical Advisor on assigned projects.
* Serves as Scientific Advisor and provides guidance to Project Leaders on the medical and scientific aspects of assigned projects.
* Provides medical support to investigative sites and project staff for protocol\-related issues including protocol clarifications, inclusion/exclusion determinations, and issues of patient safety and/or eligibility.
* Performs medical review of the protocol, Investigative Drug Brochure (IDB), and/or Case Report Forms (CRFs).
* Provides therapeutic area/indication training for the project clinical team.
* Attends and presents at Investigator Meetings.
* Performs review and clarification of trial\-related Adverse Events (AEs).
* May perform medical case review of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs), including review of case documentation and patient narrative, in collaboration with the Pharmacovigilance department.
* May provide medical support for the Analysis of Similar Events (AOSE), in collaboration with or on behalf of Pharmacovigilance department.
* May perform medical review of adverse event coding.
* Performs review of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and patient narratives.
* Attends Kick\-Off meetings, weekly team meetings, and client meetings, as needed or requested.
* Available 24/7 to respond urgent protocol \-related questions from investigative sites, in accordance with local labor laws.
* May require regular travel (15%\-20%)

**Qualifications \& Requirements*** Medical Degree from an accredited and internationally recognized medical school with a curriculum relevant to general medical education, along with a medical license, or equivalent, from the country or region in which he/she resides and works is required, plus a minimum of 5 years' experience in clinical medicine, in addition to clinical trials experience as an investigator or in the Pharma, CRO, or Biotech industry.
* Robust and current knowledge of scientific, clinical, regulatory, commercial and competitive landscape in applicable therapeutic area.
* Knowledge of applicable federal and local regulations and guidelines pertaining to clinical research.
* Business Acumen.
* Board Certified in Cardiology.
* Knowledge of cardiology, metabolic diseases and nephrology.
* Experience or knowledge in Phase I studies or early clinical development.
* Fluent English.
* Extensive communication skills.


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights, and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com.
IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.
At IQVIA, we believe that diversity, inclusion, and belonging empower our mission to accelerate innovation for a healthier world. We create a culture of belonging by valuing the perspectives of all talented employees worldwide and providing them with the opportunity to power smarter healthcare for everyone, everywhere. When our talented employees bring their authentic selves and their diverse experiences to work, they enable us to accomplish extraordinary things. Multifaceted thought processes spark innovation. Multi\-talented collaboration harnesses innovation to deliver superior outcomes. Likewise, as part of this culture, IQVIA is committed to ensuring effective equality between women and men, integrating it as a strategic principle in its corporate and human resources policies.

title: Preclinical PKPD Scientist (Client - dedicated), EMEA
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Join Us as Principal Pharmacokineticist – Make an Impact at the Forefront of Innovation  

We have successfully supported the top 50 pharmaceutical companies and more than 750 biotechs, spanning 2,700 clinical trials across 100\+ countries in the last 5 years.


As part of our expert team, you’ll have the opportunity to ensure operational excellence that makes a real difference in clinical pharmacology. You will guide design of nonclinical studies (PK, TK, PKPD), translational PKPD analysis in collaboration with the clinical Pharmacologist, support the bioanalytical scientist in strategic and operational planning of nonclinical and early clinical work.  

  

What You’ll Do:  

  

* Is responsible for managing and safeguard nonclinical PKPD data
* Interacts and discusses data and strategies with stakeholders and vendors for PKPD analysis
* Advises and sets up adequate software infrastructure in collaboration with IT
* Discusses development strategies with technical and cross\-functional teams and advises on design of nonclinical studies including GLP toxicology studies
* Reviews study reports including Toxicokinetic phase reports
* Processes and models data using appropriate software (such as Phoenix WinNonlin, R)
* Reports results to nonclinical pharmacology and bioanalytical team, cross\-functional teams and management
* Prepares documents to a quality standard required for Regulatory/Health Authority submissions
* Interacts with internal stakeholders to understand different project requirements and ensures timely, open and effective communication to relevant stakeholders (teams, collaborators, management)
* Applies model informed drug development (MIDD)

  

Education and Experience Requirements:

* Advanced degree (Master or PhD) in a scientific discipline with a strong mathematical component or equal by experience
* PKPD modelling experience in translational and early phases of development. Experience in different disease areas is a plus

  

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:* Basic understanding of GLP requirements for nonclinical safety studies
* Knowledge of regulatory requirements to characterize the nonclinical pharmakinetics and safety of new biologic entities and how to propose and justify human starting doses
* Strong communication skills
* Strong collaborative and interpersonal skills
* Problem solving skills
* Fluent in English

  

Why Join Us?  

  

When you join Thermo Fisher Scientific, you become part of a global team that values passion, innovation, and a commitment to scientific excellence. You’ll work in an environment where collaboration and development are part of the everyday experience—and where your contributions truly make a difference.  

  

Apply today to help us deliver tomorrow’s breakthroughs.

title: VP and General Partner, Strategic Partnerships, Biotech Solutions
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


We have a vacancy for **VP and General Partner, Strategic Partnerships** in our **Biotech Solutions** Team. This role is based in the **EMEA region**.


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies. With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.


The **General Partner** is responsible for leading strategic partnerships with key biotech accounts (\>$500M backlog). This role drives growth strategies across clinical (CRG) and manufacturing (PSG) service lines, ensuring positive differentiation of Thermo Fisher’s offerings from drug development to commercialization. The GP will serve as the executive interface with clients—accountable for relationship health, delivery excellence, advancement of the partnership, and overall satisfaction—while ensuring alignment with Thermo Fisher’s culture and values.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Strategic Leadership**

* Develop and execute growth strategies across CRG and PSG, leveraging Thermo Fisher’s scale, systems, and AI/digital capabilities.
* Act as executive sponsor for client partnerships, cultivating trust at senior levels and identifying expansion opportunities.
* Champion innovation and transformation to enhance R\&D efficiency and operational scalability.

**Client \& Relationship Management**

* Serve as primary executive contact for assigned accounts, ensuring delivery success and client satisfaction.
* Advocate for clients within internal teams while balancing BPS business objectives.
* Drive senior\-level engagement to deepen loyalty, build trust, and expand partnerships.

**Commercial \& Financial Performance**

* Own account performance, driving revenue growth, utilization, and profitability (including EBITDA targets).
* Lead cross\-functional teams through proposals, bid defenses, and commercial negotiations ensuring strategic differentiation and harmonized delivery
* Align divisional and functional stakeholders to deliver on financial and operational goals.

**Operational Excellence**

* Partner with delivery and resourcing teams to ensure milestones are met with high quality.
* Optimize operating models for efficiency and scalability.
* Use client insights to inform investment strategies, continuous improvement, and digital innovation.

**Team Leadership**

* Build, coach, and inspire high\-performing account teams.
* Foster a collaborative, client\-focused culture across functions.
* Serve as role model of Thermo Fisher’s Four I’s: Integrity, Intensity, Innovation, Involvement.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in business, life sciences, or related field required; MBA or advanced degree preferred.
* 15\+ years of commercial leadership in CRO, pharma, or medical device sectors, with at least 5\+ years in sales or general management.
* Proven track record in global account management and delivering growth.
* Deep knowledge of CRO industry, clinical development processes, and regulatory requirements.
* Strong executive presence, communication, and negotiation skills.
* Demonstrated ability to manage complexity, drive strategic solutions, and build lasting customer relationships.

**Working Conditions**

* Remote with frequent domestic and international travel.
* Flexible to manage both long\-term strategic projects and urgent operational needs.

**About Thermo Fisher Scientific**


Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (NYSE: TMO) is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of approximately $40 billion. Our mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. With over 100,000 colleagues worldwide, we deliver innovative technologies, trusted brands, and comprehensive services in life sciences, diagnostics, and biopharma solutions. Learn more at thermofisher.com.

Milan, Italy

**Employment Type**
Fixed Term Full Time

**Function**
Digital

**The Role**
Curious about how AI can help build a cleaner energy future?  

The Digital Intern is part of the Data Innovation team within the Digital function and reports to the Data Innovation Lead, based in our London office, while working day\-to\-day alongside with the Managing Director in Italy and the rest of the Italian team in Milan. The Digital Intern will be the first point of contact for colleagues in Italy who use AI to make a piece of their job easier, listening to requests, understanding the underlying problem, and proposing realistic, scoped solutions. They will help the business use tools such as Microsoft Copilot, Claude and Palantir Foundry, to address their needs themselves, or build small, hands\-on solutions on their behalf, a prompt, a Claude skill, a Foundry application, or a Copilot agent. Supporting AI deployment in Italy through a number of pre\-defined pilot projects such as: a Quality Document Analyzer that automatically reviews project documentation for completeness and compliance with Sonnedix and Italian regulatory standards; a Service Buyer that turns preliminary plant layouts into bid\-ready procurement packages and scores incoming offers; and an Engineering Review tool that brings BESS and SCADA design review work in\-house.  

The Digital Intern will show colleagues in the Italian team how to use what has been built so they can maintain and iterate on it independently, and will keep a simple log of every request that comes in and what was done with it, so the Data Innovation team can spot patterns and learn what kinds of AI requests are most tractable across the Italian business. They will escalate anything that turns out to be bigger than a quick win, support the Data Innovation team in shaping how targeted AI enablement looks across Italy and the wider business, and contribute to building trust in AI across the Italian organisation by explaining technical concepts in plain language to nontechnical.  

**Desirable skills:**  

* Curiosity about AI tools and a foundational understanding of what Claude, Microsoft Copilot or similar large language models can and cannot do
* Strong written and spoken communication skills in both Italian and English, with the ability to explain technical concepts in plain language to non\-technical audiences
* Comfort with ambiguity and a pragmatic, hands\-on approach \- small, scoped wins over ambitious half\-finished projects
* Exposure to no\-code or low\-code platforms such as Microsoft Copilot, Palantir Foundry, or similar productivity and automation tools
* Foundational technical literacy \- a computer science background is welcome but not required


**We use English as our business language, please submit your CV in English.**  

  

**We deploy AI across the business, from generation and trading decisions to the everyday workflows of every team. We invest in our people’s digital fluency so everyone works alongside these tools with confidence.**  

  

**Studies show that many qualified candidates hesitate to apply unless they meet every requirement. If this role excites you, but you don’t meet every single qualification, we still encourage you to apply. We value diverse skills, perspectives, and experiences that contribute to a stronger team.** **About Us**
Sonnedix is a global renewable energy company delivering 100% clean electricity through a diversified portfolio of solar, wind, and storage assets.
  

  

With almost two decades of sustainable growth, we are committed to Powering a Bright Future, as we continue acquiring, developing, building, and operating renewable energy projects for the long\-term, delivering innovative, tailored, and fully renewable energy solutions to our customers.  

  

**We help you thrive at work and beyond:** A people\-first company, committed to offer flexibility, a family\-first approach, wellbeing and training support, and – above all – team connection and community!  

**We are tech\-fluent by design**: Technology isn’t a support function, it’s how we run. We deploy AI across the business and future\-proof your career by investing in our people’s digital fluency.
  

**We have nearly two decades of sustainable growth:** Our successful track\-record of nearly 2 decades is testament to our strong sustainable growth focus. We are a reliable partner and employer, for the long term!  

**We are truly diverse and inclusive**: A culture of listening to each other, educating ourselves, and implementing changes to improve and ensure every member of our One Team feels and knows they belong.  

  

Hear from some of our team about Sonnedix: https://www.sonnedix.com/careers  

  

**What we offer:**
* **One company, many careers** \- as the company grows, we offer the opportunity for you to develop your career with us
* An **inclusive, international working culture** with a diverse team of friendly professionals from across the world
* A commitment to **flexibility** – hybrid working, generous annual leave allocation with an additional four weeks of discretionary unpaid leave
* A **family first** company \- generous paid parental leave and support for carers
* The ability to **‘work from elsewhere’**, for up to six weeks in a year
* **Summer working hours**
* Financial, Social, Mental \& Physical **wellbeing** support
* The **Sonnedix Academy** – a range of training and development opportunities including professional development, management and leadership programs, technical training, essential skills, DEI, mentorship and much more
* Dedicated learning paths, tools, and resources to help every colleague **confidently use AI and digital tools** in their work
* **Fantastic office environments**
* Social events, **team building** opportunities and One Team Network groups, which are employee\-led groups, based on shared identities, interests, backgrounds and life experiences


and many more local benefits!

title: Data Center Cleaner
description:
**Salute is a global leader in delivering Data Center services – firmly established in the US and with a rapidly growing client base across Europe and beyond.**

**This is an exciting time to join the team as we step up operations in new countries and commence work on new and challenging projects. As a result of this growth, we are looking to recruit a Cleaner in Noviglio, Italy.**

**The Cleaner is responsible for maintaining a clean facility by completing a variety of cleaning tasks. Their duties include mopping and vacuuming floors, cleaning surfaces with disinfectants, and emptying trash cans or recycling bins.**

**The Cleaner also notifies managers concerning the need for minor or major repairs or additions to building operations. This position establishes and maintains effective communication and working relationships with clients, co\-workers, shift coordinators, supervisors, and managers. This custodial position must comply with all safety, security, compliance, and quality standards and procedures established by the company, clients, and regulatory authorities.**

**Skills/Responsibilities/Experience/Knowledge**

* Cleans building floors by sweeping, mopping, scrubbing, or vacuuming them. Follows procedures for the use of chemical cleaners and power equipment to prevent damage to floors and fixtures
* Sometimes using ladders, dust and washes furniture, walls, machines, and equipment; cleans ceilings and dust and polishes light fixtures
* Moves cabinets, boxes, furniture, crates, and equipment to clean areas, either manually or by using hand trucks
* May remove stains from such surfaces as rugs, drapes, walls, and floors using chemicals and cleaning solutions
* Strips, seals, finishes, and polishes floors
* Cleans and vacuums carpeted areas, including the application of spot cleaning treatment
* Gathers and empties trash
* Wipes and cleans tabletops, chairs, and equipment in food areas
* Cleans and polishes furniture and fixtures
* Services, cleans, and supplies restrooms
* Periodically cleans overhead vents, dust blinds, and polishes wood furniture
* Cleans interior partition glass, windows, glass partitions, and mirrors using soapy water or other cleaners, sponges, and squeegees
* Sets up, arranges, and removes decorations, tables, chairs, ladders, and scaffolding to prepare facilities for events such as banquets and meetings
* Must keep janitor’s closet neat and orderly and maintain equipment in good working order
* Requisitions supplies and equipment needed for cleaning and maintenance duties
* Monitors building security and safety by performing such tasks as locking doors after operating hours and possibly checking electrical appliance use to ensure that hazards are not created
* Miscellaneous duties as assigned

**Requirements:**

* Hands on experience in a similar role

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Drivers license
* Used to a working in a high security environment

title: Health & Safety Lead - Italy (They/She/He)
description:
Milano, Italy


Full\-time


People


  
**Who we are**
--------------


Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 70 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.


### **Job Description**


If you’re here, it’s because you’re looking for an **exciting ride**.


A ride that will fuel up your ambitions to take on a **new challenge and stretch yourself beyond your comfort zone.**


We’ll deliver a **non\-vanilla culture built on talent, where we work to amplify the impact on millions of people**, paving the way forward together.

**Not your usual app**. We are the fastest\-growing multi\-category app connecting millions of users with businesses, and couriers, offering on\-demand services from more than 170,000 local restaurants, grocers and supermarkets, and high street retail stores. We operate in more than 1500 cities across 23 countries.


Together we revolutionise the way people connect with their everyday needs, from delivering essentials to connecting our ecosystem of users through innovative solutions powered by technology. For us, every day is filled with purpose.

**What makes our ride unique?**

**Our culture and strong values.**

**Our career development philosophy.**

**Our commitment to being a force for good.**  



We have a vision: **Building the largest marketplace in your city, to give access to anything in minutes****.** And this is where your ride starts.

**YOUR MISSION**


We’re looking for a **Health \& Safety Lead** to join our People team in **Italy**. If you thrive in fast\-paced environments, are passionate about safety culture, regulatory excellence, and have the ability to lead both processes and people, this is your place!


You will play a crucial role in ensuring compliance with Italian health \& safety legislation, developing and implementing local processes, and managing internal and external stakeholders, all while fostering a safe and productive work environment.

**THE JOURNEY**

* Manage and control all Health and Safety matters according to **Italian law**, ensuring full **regulatory compliance**
* Serve as the company's **RSPP (Responsabile del Servizio di Prevenzione e Protezione)**, taking ownership of all related legal responsibilities
* Lead and ensure proper documentation and records management to support labor inspections
* Create, implement, and continuously improve **H\&S processes and protocols** at both technical and organizational levels
* Lead or support the **investigation of occupational incidents**, identifying root causes and recommending corrective actions
* Develop and deliver **H\&S training programs** and track completion rates across the organization
* Coordinate **health surveillance programs** in accordance with applicable regulations
* Manage **emergency planning**, including the design and implementation of evacuation procedures
* Handle **Coordination of Business Activities (Coordinamento delle Attività Imprenditoriali – D.Lgs. 81/08 Art. 26\)** and liaise with contractors and third\-party providers
* Provide clear guidance and support to stakeholders, including leadership, on H\&S matters
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams, including Facilities, Operations, and Legal, to integrate safety into all areas of the business
* **Mentor and guide** one junior H\&S resource, supporting their professional growth


### **Qualifications**


**WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE**

* **RSPP Qualification** in accordance with Italian legislation (D.Lgs. 81/08\)
* Strong knowledge of **Italian H\&S regulations** and experience applying them in a corporate or operational environment
* Proven experience in **creating and implementing Health \& Safety processes**
* Solid track record of **stakeholder management**, including communication with leadership and external authorities
* Experience working in **high\-pressure, fast\-paced environments**, preferably in logistics, manufacturing, or tech/startup industries
* Demonstrated experience in:
	+ Accident investigation
	+ Emergency planning
	+ CAE platforms (Coordinamento Attività Esterne)
	+ Risk assessment and mitigation planning
	+ Work inspections
* Strong organizational and analytical skills
* Hands\-on attitude and the ability to work independently
* **Professional proficiency in English** (written and spoken)
* Comfortable with travel and site visits across Italy as needed
* Advanced **Excel / Google Sheets skills**; experience with JavaScript for automation or reporting is a strong plus


Individuals representing diverse profiles, **and abilities,** encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! **Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.**

**We believe driven talent deserves:**

* An enticing equity plan that lets you own a piece of the action.
* Top\-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
* Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
* ️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
* Enhanced parental leave
* Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well\-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds \- all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.


Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

**So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?**


Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

title: Head of Innovation
description:
**Who We Are**

At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission\-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world’s leading businesses. We are at the heart of progress; with proven expertise and a continuous flow of AI\-powered insight, enabling smarter decisions, faster innovation, and a lasting competitive edge. For our people—Kyndryls—that means doing purposeful work that powers human progress. Join us and experience a flexible, supportive environment where your well\-being is prioritized and your potential can thrive.  



  

**The Role**

Intesa, a Kyndryl Group company, is looking for a **Head of Innovation** as part of its growth strategy and efforts to position itself as a hub for innovation and digital transformation.
  

**Role Mission**  

Lead the identification, design, and go\-to\-market strategy for new digital services, while accelerating the structural adoption of AI within the organization.
  

**Key Responsibilities**  

*Service Innovation*

* Continuous analysis of technological, regulatory, and market trends to identify opportunities for new services aligned with Intesa’s positioning
* Defining business cases, MVPs, and launch roadmaps for new solutions, in coordination with Product, Compliance, and Technology
* Managing the idea\-to\-market cycle: from validation with pilot customers to production rollout

*AI Evangelism \& Adoption*

* Promoting an AI culture within the company through training, demos, internal use cases, and communities of practice
* Scouting and evaluating AI tools, models, and frameworks applicable to internal processes and services offered
* Supporting business units in identifying high\-impact AI use cases and measuring results

**Requirements:**

* High school diploma or bachelor’s degree in Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
* At least 3–5 years of experience in this role
* Excellent knowledge of English; a minimum B2–C1 level is required
* Goal\-oriented mindset and strong customer focus
* Excellent mediation skills, even in critical situations
* Problem\-solving skills and a structured approach to decision\-making

**Location: Turin\-Milan (with a remote work contract)**  

**What we offer:**

* A young and dynamic environment
* A highly flexible remote work contract
* Trust and respect for ideas and opinions: in our team, everyone is free to share and propose new ideas and solutions
* Community events and initiatives where you’ll have the opportunity to discuss company projects related to sustainability, inclusion, and education.

***Consent to data processing***  

*You can withdraw your consent at any time by sending an e\-mail to the following address: privacy\_mktg@intesa.it. Or, if you no longer wish to receive marketing e\-mails, you can unsubscribe by clicking on the relevant unsubscribe link in any e\-mail. Further information on procedures can be found in the INTESA Privacy Policy. By submitting this form, I declare that I have read and understood the INTESA Privacy Policy.*  

***Diversity\&Inclusion***  

*HR managers, HR recruiters and employees all have a responsibility to ensure that diversity and inclusion are respected during the selection process. Prejudices and unconscious stereotypes must not influence our selection processes. We look for the best candidates and the real value they can bring to the organisation. We value meritocracy. We value diversity, setting aside our prejudices. All persons should have the opportunity to be considered for employment regardless of their race, colour, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, military service, pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, or any other characteristic protected by law and our collective bargaining agreements. INTESA will endeavour to make reasonable accommodation of known physical or mental limitations of a qualified candidate with a disability, unless the accommodation would impose an undue hardship on the operation of our business.*  

  

***Acknowledgement of the Code of Ethics and Disciplinary Code***  

*Before submitting your application, you are invited to read the company's Code of Ethics, which can be found on our website, and to note that Intesa's disciplinary code complies with the relevant provisions of the CCNL metalmeccanici, the collective agreement for employees and managers of Intesa s.p.a.*

  

**Who You Are**

See the job description above  



  

**Being You**

The “Kyn” in Kyndryl means kinship, which represents the strong bonds we have with each other, our customers and our communities. We focus on ensuring all Kyndryls feel included and we welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply. We believe in growth, and we’re excited to see what you can bring. At Kyndryl, employee feedback has told us that our number one driver of employee engagement is belonging. That sense of belonging — being a valued, respected, trusted member of the team — is fundamental to our culture and fueling great experiences for our customers. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you the ability to thrive and contribute to our culture of empathy and shared success. That’s The Kyndryl Way.  



  

**What You Can Expect**

Your career with us isn’t just a job—it’s an adventure with purpose. We offer a dynamic, hybrid\-friendly culture that supports your well\-being and empowers you to grow. Our Be Well programs are thoughtfully designed to support your financial, mental, physical, and social health—because we know that when you feel your best, you do your best.
  

  

From your very first day, you’ll dive into impactful work that powers the systems our customers rely on every day. You won’t just contribute—you’ll make a difference, tackling meaningful projects that sharpen your skills and fuel your growth.
  

  

We’re here to champion your journey. With powerful tools to chart your career path, personalized development goals aligned with your ambitions, and continuous feedback to keep you inspired and on track, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and evolve. You’ll develop in\-demand skills to grow your career and achieve your ambitions with access to cutting\-edge learning opportunities—from certifications with Microsoft, Google, and Amazon to coaching and hands\-on experiences. And through it all, you’ll be part of a culture that values empathy, restless learning, and a devotion to shared success.
  

  

We want you to thrive here—and we’re committed to helping you do just that. Ready to make an impact? Join us and help shape what’s next.


**Get Referred!**

If you know someone that works at Kyndryl, when asked ‘How Did You Hear About Us’ during the application process, select ‘Employee Referral’ and enter your contact's Kyndryl email address.

title: Senior Legal Compliance & Regulatory Counsel (m/f/d)
description:
Where passion meets premium – join our team!

 **Location:** Milano, Italy

**Employment Type:** FULL TIME, ONSITE

**This role is open to candidates based in Zug (Switzerland), Barcelona (Spain), London (UK), Milano (Italy), or Kirchheim (Germany).**
About Le Creuset


At Le Creuset, every dish begins with a story \- and every story begins with people. Since 1925, we’ve brought generations together around the table, transforming everyday meals into cherished memories. Our legacy is built on colour, craft, and a deep passion for innovation \- shaped as much by our iconic products as by the people who bring them to life. We’re not just creating cookware; we’re creating moments that last a lifetime. If you’re inspired by heritage, driven by creativity, and ready to shape the future of the kitchen, we’d love to welcome you into our story.
The Recipe for Success: Role Summary


The Senior Legal Compliance \& Regulatory Counsel serves as a trusted expert and strategic partner to the business, providing authoritative legal and regulatory guidance to ensure the organisation’s activities comply with applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards. The role focuses on designing, implementing, and continuously improving the compliance framework while enabling the business to meet its strategic objectives in a risk\-aware and legally sound manner. The main activities will focus on product and packaging regulations, sustainability, internal compliance policies (anti\-bribery, whistleblowing, code of conduct, etc) and third party suppliers policies.
Ingredients: Key Responsibilities**Regulatory Governance \& Advisory**

* Act as a senior subject‑matter expert on legal and regulatory compliance, advising senior management and key stakeholders on complex and high‑risk regulatory matters.
* Interpret and assess the impact of existing and emerging laws, regulations, guidance, and enforcement trends.
* Provide pragmatic, business‑oriented legal advice balancing regulatory requirements with commercial objectives.

**Compliance Framework \& Policies**

* Design, develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive legal and regulatory compliance framework.
* Draft, review, and update compliance policies, standards, procedures, and codes of conduct.
* Support regulatory change management and horizon scanning activities.

**Risk Management \& Oversight**

* Conduct compliance risk assessments, gap analyses, and control reviews.
* Maintain compliance risk registers and remediation plans.
* Advise on compliance risks related to new products, services, and strategic initiatives.
* Regulatory Engagement \& Investigations
* Act as a key liaison with external advisors.
* Support internal investigations into potential compliance breaches.

**Training \& Culture**

* Develop and deliver compliance training programmes.
* Promote a strong culture of ethics and compliance across the organisation.

The Perfect Blend:
  

What We’re Looking For
Essential

* Fully qualified lawyer (or equivalent legal qualification) with significant post‑qualification experience in legal and regulatory compliance.
* Strong experience advising senior leaders on complex regulatory and compliance matters within a regulated or multinational environment.
* Mandatory: Experience with physical **product, packaging, sustainability,** and supply‑chain related regulations.
* Proven ability to design, implement, and maintain compliance frameworks, policies, and governance structures.
* Sound judgement with a pragmatic, business‑oriented approach to balancing legal risk and commercial objectives.
* Experience conducting compliance risk assessments and advising on mitigation strategies.
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively across multiple functions.
* High ethical standards and a strong commitment to fostering a culture of integrity and compliance.
* Experience supporting regulatory matters, internal investigations, or compliance training initiatives.

Why Join Our Table?
At Le Creuset, you will find your seat at our table, which is as colourful as our cookware and rich in history. Our table is grounded in our values: **Passion, Integrity, Excellence, Innovation and Teamwork.**


Every talent will add flavor. Every idea brings colour. And every day is a chance to create something that lasts. With the passion you bring, you become part of a timeless legacy, one that brings people together around food, stories, and meaningful shared moments.
Diversity and Inclusion Statement*We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, race, ethnic or national origin. We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to reach their full potential.*

title: Sustainability Consultant
description:
Arcadis is the world's leading company delivering sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy solutions for natural and built assets.



We are more than 34,000 people, in over 30 countries, dedicated to improving quality of life. Everyone has an important role to play. With the power of many curious minds, together we can solve the world’s most complex challenges and deliver more impact together.


**Role description:**

  



The candidate will become part of the **Smart Sustainable Buildings** team with the role of Senior Sustainability Consultant.  

The ideal candidate has previous experience in MEP or Mechanical design, and will support colleagues thanks to their practical experience in managing projects related to energy efficiency for the industrial and tertiary sectors.

  


**Role accountabilities:**


* Collaborate with the team lead in managing projects from the pursuit\-to\-win stage to the delivery\-to\-result.
* Lead specific project teams in reference to sustainability assessments and building certifications for client organizations.
* Develop and implement sustainability strategies and action plans.
* Analyze data related to energy use, waste, water, and emissions.
* Advise clients on regulatory compliance and industry best practices.
* Prepare detailed reports and present findings to stakeholders.
* Lead workshops and training sessions on sustainability topics.
* Implement and develop certifications activities (BREEAM \& LEED consultancy), decarbonization projects (SBTi, LCA, \& CRREM assessments) and EU Taxonomy (compliance assessments);
* Lead projects during the design and construction stage
* Collaborate with your colleagues of inter\-disciplinary teams for technical due diligence and design assessments

  


**Qualifications \& Experience:**

  


* **Around 5\-6 years of proven work experience** in consulting activities in sustainability sector, **with previous experience in MEP / Mechanical building design required**;
* **A degree in Mechanical, Electrical or Civil Engineering** (other engineering backgrounds would be accepted in case of relevant experience).
* **At least one AP Sustainability credentials**.
* **An excellent (B2\) level of English and Italian**
* Strong knowledge of sustainability frameworks (LEED AP, BREAM AP, WELL AP etc.).
* Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Strong written and verbal communication abilities.
* Project management experience is a plus.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
* Proficiency in data analysis and reporting tools.
* Up\-to\-date knowledge of environmental regulations and standards.
* Passion for sustainability and environmental stewardship.
* Availability for business trips on the national / international territory

  


**Nice to have:**


* EGE Certification
* Project management certification PMP or similar;
* Experience in design activities
* Good knowledge of LEVELs framework and other international energy\-environmental protocols

  


**Why Arcadis?**



We can only achieve our goals when everyone is empowered to be their best. We believe everyone's contribution matters. It’s why we are pioneering a skills\-based approach, where you can harness your unique experience and expertise to carve your career path and maximize the impact we can make together.



You’ll do meaningful work, and no matter what role, you’ll be helping to deliver sustainable solutions for a more prosperous planet. Make your mark, on your career, your colleagues, your clients, your life and the world around you.



Together, we can create a lasting legacy.



Join Arcadis. Create a Legacy.


**Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging**



We want you to be able to bring your best self to work every day, which is why we take equality and inclusion seriously and hold ourselves to account for our actions. Our ambition is to be an employer of choice and provide a great place to work for all our people.

  

\#LI\-GS1



\#LI\-Hybrid

title: MARKET & REGULATORY SCENARIOS JUNIOR ANALYST
description:
Unisciti a Edison!


Siamo leader nella **transizione energetica** , con una storia di innovazione lunga 140 anni.


Cerchiamo un/a **Market \& Regulatory Scenarios Junio Analyst.**

Nell’ambito della *Direzione Strategy \& Corporate Development* , la risorsa sarà parte del *team Market \& Regulatory Scenarios* con il compito di sviluppare, attraverso un modello di mercato, gli scenari energetici di medio e lungo periodo per l’azienda, con particolare riferimento all’evoluzione del mix energetico e degli usi finali e all’andamento delle principali commodity energetiche e ambientali. Il focus principale sarà effettuato sull’Italia con una overview generale di altri paesi europei di particolare rilevanza. La risorsa dovrà poi analizzare le principali novità di mercato, tecnologiche e regolatorie, individuando i macro trend del settore energetico e delineandone i principali impatti sulle attività di Edison presenti e future. La risorsa dovrà infine realizzare analisi qualitative e quantitative per il top management sui temi di principale interesse/rilevanza per la società in tutti i business in cui opera.


**Cosa farai nel quotidiano**

* + trend di settore e analisi di mercato
	+ analisi di impatto dell’evoluzione normativa sul lungo periodo
	+ supporto nello sviluppo dei diversi business attraverso un’analisi critica del contesto di mercato
	+ scenari di domanda e prezzo delle commodities e dei servizi energetici

**Chi sei**

* + Laurea Magistrale in Ingegneria, preferibile ingegneria energetica
	+ Capacità di reperire e analizzare informazioni in modo critico
	+ Capacità analitiche, accompagnate da una buona dose di curiosità e proattività
	+ Capacità comunicative in contesti inter\-funzionali e di lavoro in team
	Capacità di comunicazione, scritta e orale, anche in lingua inglese, che consentano di relazionarsi con persone di diverso livello e/o estrazione  
	+

**Come funziona il processo di selezione**

Il nostro processo di selezione prevede colloqui motivazionali e tecnici e, per i profili junior, anche test di personalità e di logica deduttiva. Garantiamo l'equità in tutte le fasi del processo di assunzione, monitorando l'"Adverse Impact", un sistema di valutazione dal quale emerge l’assenza di biais di genere  



**Perché scegliere Edison?**

* **Crescita personale e professionale** : le competenze apprese sul lavoro, la community di tecnici ed esperti e la crescita individuale sono il primo fattore per cui i tuoi futuri colleghi continuano a sceglierci
* **Work\-life balance** : smart working, benefit aziendali e iniziative per il benessere mentale e fisico delle nostre persone sono al centro di un sistema di welfare flessibile e capace di rispondere a bisogni diversi: dalla genitorialità alla salute, dalla mobilità sostenibile alla conciliazione vita\-lavoro
* **Giovani e innovazione** : Potrai entrare nella nostra Young Community, un percorso volto a scoprire l'azienda e i suoi business, caratterizzato da momenti di formazione e sviluppo, socializzazione e networking. Con il piano company social housing diamo ai neoassunti neolaureati l’opportunità di una casa a costi accessibili in prossimità della sede di lavoro
* **Energia che cambia tutto** : lavora in un settore chiave per il futuro dell’ambiente e lascia la tua impronta per costruire un domani migliore

Siamo la società leader dell’energia, con oltre 140 anni di storia e primati che fanno di noi il più antico operatore del settore in Europa. Impieghiamo oltre 6\.000 persone, operando in Italia ed Europa nella produzione rinnovabile e low carbon, nell’approvvigionamento e vendita di gas naturale, nella mobilità sostenibile, e attraverso Edison Energia ed Edison Next nei servizi energetici, ambientali e a valore aggiunto per clienti, aziende, territori e Pubblica Amministrazione. Come Gruppo siamo impegnati in prima linea nella sfida della transizione energetica, in coerenza con i Sustainable Development Goals dell’Onu e le politiche europee di decarbonizzazione.


*Unisciti al \#futuroincorso!*

Candidati all’offerta di lavoro cliccando il bottone in alto.


*Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi del decreto legislativo 11 aprile 2006 n. 198 “Codice delle pari opportunità tra uomo e donna” e a persone di tutte le età e nazionalità, ai sensi della Direttiva 2000/78/CE “Parità di trattamento in materia di occupazione”.*

**Cerchi aiuto?** Contattaci qui: *talentacquisition@edison.it*

title: Experienced - Finance Transformation Accounting&Regulatory Insurance
description:
### **Job Specs**

**Job Name**Experienced \- Finance Transformation Accounting\&Regulatory Insurance


**Location**Milano


**Business Area**FINANCE TRANSFORMATION


**Seniority**Experienced


**Business Function**TECHNOLOGY \& TRANSFORMATION


### **What impact will you make?**

**Experienced \- Finance Transformation Accounting\&Regulatory Insurance**  

 Stiamo cercando figure Experienced da inserire nel team Finance Transformation nelle nostre principali sedi, con opportunità di lavorare in modalità Hybrid.  

 Il business Finance Transformation si occupa di supportare i nostri clienti nella individuazione di soluzioni innovative, finalizzate alla trasformazione dei processi e delle tecnologie a supporto delle attività produttive dell’area CFO.  

 Siamo alla ricerca di professionisti con esperienza e spiccate capacità manageriali. Il candidato ideale dovrebbe possedere esperienza e conoscenze delle tematiche Accounting e Regulatory Reporting, analisi dei dati e tecnologie emergenti, con particolare focus in ambito Insurance.  

 **\#Your Role**  

 Cerchiamo una risorsa che desideri unirsi a un team di consulenti all'avanguardia nel settore, focalizzato al supporto dell’area CFO nell’ambito dei processi e sistemi contabili e di vigilanza. Le responsabilità principali sono:* Reingegnerizzazione e disegno di processi e modelli operativi target

* Analisi funzionale e supporto all’execution dei processi in ambito accounting e reporting

* Definizione dei business requirement finalizzati all’evoluzione dei sistemi di sintesi segnaletici e/o contabili

* Collaborare attivamente con i team interni e i nostri clienti per comprendere le esigenze aziendali e tradurli in opportunità di business

* Contribuire attivamente alla crescita aziendale attraverso la condivisione delle conoscenze, la formazione e il coinvolgimento in iniziative di sviluppo aziendale

  

**\#Winning Requirements*** Laurea magistrale in Economia, Ingegneria Gestionale o equivalenti
* Esperienza consolidata in Società di Consulenza o Compagnie di assicurazione, avendo maturato competenze e conoscenze in ambito:

* Regulatory Reporting ( Solvency, etc.)
* Accounting, Principi IAS/IFRS (es. IFRS 9, IFRS 17, etc.)
* Process Re\-engineering
* Financial Reporting

* Capacità di lavorare in team, con un approccio proattivo alla problem solving
* Esperienza nella gestione di complessi programmi di trasformazione, garantendo il rispetto dei tempi e budget
* Eccellenti capacità analitiche e attenzione ai dettagli, con una mentalità orientata al raggiungimento degli obiettivi
* Disponibilità a trasferte in Italia e all’estero
* Fluente conoscenza della lingua inglese

 **To Make an Impact that Matters****Il network Deloitte, leader nei servizi professionali alle imprese, opera a livello globale in più di 150 Paesi con oltre 460 mila persone.**


In Italia, Deloitte è presente in 24 città con circa 13\.400 persone che assistono i clienti offrendo servizi di Audit \& Assurance, Technology \& Transformantion, Strategy, Risk \& Transactions Advisory e Tax \& Legal.

  

In qualità di network globale ci impegniamo per affrontare le principali sfide sociali, economiche e ambientali della società di oggi. **World*****Impact***è il nostro programma globale che riunisce tutte le iniziative attraverso cui vogliamo avere un impatto positivo sulle persone, i clienti, l’ambiente e le comunità in cui operiamo.


Con**Impact for Italy**, la declinazione italiana di **World*****Impact*****, Deloitte ha realizzato** progetti che – in collaborazione con istituzioni, realtà del terzo settore, università e altri soggetti – hanno lasciato un impatto concreto e una *legacy* per il futuro su tutto il territorio nazionale.

  

Sei pront\* a \#MakeAnImpactThatMatter insieme? Candidati ora!  

  

 La ricerca rispetta il d.lgs. 198/2006, il D.lgs. 215/03 e il D.lgs. 216/03 ed è aperta a candidat\* di qualsiasi orientamento o espressione di genere, orientamento sessuale, età, etnia e credo religioso. Il presente annuncio è stato ideato nel rispetto della diversity e dell’inclusività.
company_description: We’re a leading professional services firm, but what makes working at Deloitte unique? When you join us, you become connected. To inspiring people. To meaningful work with fascinating clients.
